title: Steven Fenyves, CFP, CFS - Valued Wealth Management Inc.
name: Steven Fenyves, CFP, CFS
details:
**Succession:** 21 years in practice | Solo practice with small team | Named after service ("Valued Wealth")
**AUM:** $50M-150M** (based on solo practice with 2 employees, 21 yea
**Why:** Solo founder (no partnership complexity) | 21 years = mature practice, peak value | Independent BD affiliation (Osaic) = portable book
approach:
Opener:

**Subject:** "21 years in Boca—what's your succession plan?"

Steven,

I came across Valued Wealth Management and saw you founded the practice in 2005. That's 21 years serving Boca Raton professionals—impressive longevity in a competitive market.

I'm reaching out because I work with advisors in similar positions: solo practice owners who've built something valuable over 20+ years and are now thinking about what the next 5-10 years look like.

Most tell me they're great at retirement pl

title: Mauro Guerra - Guerra Wealth Advisors

details:

Mauro,

I came across Guerra Wealth Advisors and was impressed by the 40-year legacy you've built serving South Florida families. From immigrant entrepreneur to $600M+ practice—that's an incredible story.

title: Alvaro Acevedo Jr., JD, CPA - Brickell Accountants
name: Alvaro Acevedo Jr., JD, CPA

triggerEvent: High-income entrepreneurs ($500K+ income) with Act 60 Puerto Rico tax optimization and multi-entity structuring. No advisor partnership mentioned. Bil
details: High-income entrepreneurs ($500K+ income) with Act 60 Puerto Rico tax optimization and multi-entity structuring. No advisor partnership mentioned. Bilingual practice serving global entrepreneurs with international footprint.
advisorApproach: Victor, I came across your work with high-income entrepreneurs and Act 60 relocations—impressive niche. I'm a wealth advisor in Miami working with similar clients (business owners scaling 7-8 figures). I'm finding most CPAs focus on compliance, not strategy. You clearly do both. Curious if you've built referral relationships with advisors who complement your tax work—estate planning, investment structuring, insurance optimization?

triggerEvent: Construction contractors, freight companies, wholesale distributors with complex cash flow. 20+ years experience, Big 4 background. Strong tax resolut
details: Construction contractors, freight companies, wholesale distributors with complex cash flow. 20+ years experience, Big 4 background. Strong tax resolution practice. No advisor partnership on website.
advisorApproach: Edgar, I saw you've built a strong practice with construction and freight businesses in Miami. Those clients have unique cash flow challenges. I'm finding business owners in those industries often have great tax planning but terrible wealth accumulation strategies. They're profitable, but not building personal wealth outside the business. Curious if you've seen that pattern and whether there's a fit to collaborate?

triggerEvent: Startups, real estate investors, and high-net-worth individuals. International tax planning. 500+ LinkedIn connections (well-networked). Real estate i
details: Startups, real estate investors, and high-net-worth individuals. International tax planning. 500+ LinkedIn connections (well-networked). Real estate investors over-concentrated in property, startup founders in equity.
advisorApproach: Edward, I saw you work with real estate investors and startup founders in Miami—two groups with similar blind spots: great at building wealth, terrible at protecting and diversifying it. Real estate guys have 100% in property. Startup founders have 100% in equity. Both need someone to help them translate that into long-term financial security. Worth a quick call to explore?

triggerEvent: International business owners, foreign investors in U.S. real estate (FIRPTA), FATCA compliance. Serving clients since 1980 (44 years). Attorney + CPA
details: International business owners, foreign investors in U.S. real estate (FIRPTA), FATCA compliance. Serving clients since 1980 (44 years). Attorney + CPA dual expertise. No wealth advisor partnership despite ultra-HNW international clients.
advisorApproach: Alvaro, I saw you've been serving international clients in Brickell since 1980—serious track record. I keep running into foreign nationals who have great tax and legal structuring but zero investment strategy or wealth planning. They're compliant and structured, but not optimized for long-term wealth growth. Curious if you've seen that pattern and whether there's a fit to collaborate on the international client segment?

triggerEvent: IRS problem resolution (clients in financial distress), financial modeling, expert witness testimony. Virtual firm, tech-forward. Financial coaching m
details: IRS problem resolution (clients in financial distress), financial modeling, expert witness testimony. Virtual firm, tech-forward. Financial coaching mentioned (stepping into advisory territory). No wealth advisor partnership.
advisorApproach: I noticed Mongio & Associates specializes in IRS problem resolution—valuable niche. I'm curious: what happens to your clients AFTER you fix their tax problems? They're compliant again, but do they have a plan to rebuild wealth and avoid ending up back in the same position? I work with business owners who've been through financial crises. The ones who succeed long-term have two things: a great CPA and a wealth advisor. Worth a conversation about the 'what comes next' side?

triggerEvent: Founded 1986 (40 years). Age 65-75 estimated. Family succession with sons Sebastian and Fabian now in practice. $600M+ AUM. Classic patriarch transiti
details: Founded 1986 (40 years). Age 65-75 estimated. Family succession with sons Sebastian and Fabian now in practice. $600M+ AUM. Classic patriarch transition scenario.
advisorApproach: Mauro, I saw Guerra Wealth Advisors has been serving Miami families since 1986—incredible 40-year legacy. I noticed your sons Sebastian and Fabian have joined the practice. As someone who's built a $600M+ firm, are you exploring continuity options beyond family succession? Many founders consider strategic partnerships to ensure client continuity. Worth a conversation about what succession could look like?

triggerEvent: Founded 2005 (21 years). Boca Raton. Solo practice (2 employees per RocketReach). Affiliated with Osaic Wealth (formerly Securities America). Age like
details: Founded 2005 (21 years). Boca Raton. Solo practice (2 employees per RocketReach). Affiliated with Osaic Wealth (formerly Securities America). Age likely 55-65. Hofstra grad, served South Florida 21 years.
advisorApproach: Steven, I saw you founded Valued Wealth Management in 2005—21 years serving Boca Raton professionals is impressive. As a solo/small practice owner approaching the 20-25 year mark, have you started thinking about succession planning? Many advisors your age are exploring strategic partnerships to ensure client continuity while maximizing practice value. Would you be open to a confidential conversation about what your next 5-10 years look like?

triggerEvent: Founded 2006 (20 years). Boca Raton. Partnership with Richard Koblick (CRPC, AWMA). 20-year milestone, partnership structure creates succession comple
details: Founded 2006 (20 years). Boca Raton. Partnership with Richard Koblick (CRPC, AWMA). 20-year milestone, partnership structure creates succession complexity. 'Old-school' relationship-focused practice.
advisorApproach: Josh, I noticed you and Richard founded Feller Financial in 2006—20 years serving South Florida. As partners approaching the 20-year mark, have you two discussed succession planning? Many partnerships hit complexity at this stage (who exits first, how to value each partner's book). I work with advisors in similar positions exploring strategic partnerships that solve the succession puzzle. Worth a conversation?

triggerEvent: Founded 2015 (11 years). Coral Gables. Independent RIA serving U.S. and international clients. Too young for immediate succession but good future pipe
details: Founded 2015 (11 years). Coral Gables. Independent RIA serving U.S. and international clients. Too young for immediate succession but good future pipeline target.
advisorApproach: Jaime, I saw you founded Angelus Investment Consulting in 2015 serving U.S. and international clients from Coral Gables. While succession may be years away, many advisors tell me they wish they'd started planning earlier. As your practice matures (11 years now), have you thought about long-term continuity planning? Not looking to buy today—just exploring whether there's fit for future partnership. Worth keeping in touch?

triggerEvent: Founded 2000 as TBK Investments, sold to major bank 2007 (GenSpring), re-established as independent WE Family Offices 2013. Now serves 80+ UHNW famili
details: Founded 2000 as TBK Investments, sold to major bank 2007 (GenSpring), re-established as independent WE Family Offices 2013. Now serves 80+ UHNW families, $14.1B in assets advised/reported. Too large and institutionalized for typical acquisition, but good partnership/alliance target.
advisorApproach: Santiago, I saw WE Family Offices serves 80+ UHNW families with $14B+ advised—impressive scale. I'm curious about your journey from founding TBK in 2000, selling to a bank, then re-establishing independence in 2013. That's rare. As someone who's been through both sides (selling and building), would you be open to sharing insights on what you learned? Not looking to pitch anything—just learning from someone who's navigated the path I'm exploring.
